revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=Ministry of Interior Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City Archaeological Survey Team|title=Tokyo Tama City Archaeological Sites: Wada-Mogusa Sites and Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Tama City|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=Ministry of Interior Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City Archaeological Survey Team|title=Tokyo Tama City Archaeological Sites: Wada-Mogusa Sites and Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Tama City|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}

revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=Ministry of Interior Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City Archaeological Survey Team|title=Tokyo Tama City Archaeological Sites: Wada-Mogusa Sites and Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Tama City|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=Ministry of Interior Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}

revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=Ministry of Interior Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City Archaeological Survey Team|title=Tokyo Tama City Archaeological Sites: Wada-Mogusa Sites and Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Tama City|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=Ministry of Interior Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City Archaeological Survey Team|title=Tokyo Tama City Archaeological Sites: Wada-Mogusa Sites and Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Tama City|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}} |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Construction period=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |Excavated artifacts=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji ware, Sue ware, etc. |Burial facilities={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Historic designation=None |Cultural property designation=None |Size=Over 10 {{ill|Circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of burial mounds from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the burial mounds have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|ja|石室}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular burial mounds|ja|円墳}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', “40–50 burial mounds existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more burial mounds. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue ware, Haji ware, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular burial mound with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji ware jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. Excavated artifacts include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji ware, and Sue ware.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The burial mound and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular burial mound with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue ware imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the burial mound nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular burial mound with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
