'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|12=draft}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=draft}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=draft}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市|12=draft}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=draft}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅|12=draft}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学|12=draft}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|12=draft}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=draft}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=draft}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市|12=draft}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=draft}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅|12=draft}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学|12=draft}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image募集中|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=draft}} |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |excavations=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji pottery, Sue pottery, etc. |Burial Facility={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |Historic Site=None |Historic Site=None |area=Over 10 {{ill|Circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |map={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. Numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|12=draft}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=draft}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=draft}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hino City|ja|日野市|12=draft}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=draft}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|ja|多摩センター駅|12=draft}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|Tachi sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学|12=draft}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=塚原古墳群 |別名= |画像={{画像募集中|cat=多摩市}} |画像キャプション= |所在地=[[東京都]][[多摩市]][[和田 (多摩市)|和田]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=[[6世紀]]中頃から[[7世紀]]中頃 |出土品=武器・武具・装身具・土師器・須恵器など |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財=未指定 |規模=[[円墳]]10基以上 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=塚原古墳群|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=都内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''塚原古墳群'''（つかっぱらこふんぐん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]][[和田 (多摩市)|和田]]にある[[古墳時代]]後期（[[6世紀]]中頃から[[7世紀]]中頃）の[[古墳群]]である。多くの[[副葬品]]が出土している。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期の古墳群で、現在10基の[[古墳]]が確認されている。現在、1号墳以外は墳丘は残っておらず、調査は[[石室]]、[[周溝]]の[[発掘調査]]となっている。埋葬者は不明である。
全てが[[横穴式石室]]による[[円墳]]と推測されるが、石室が確認されているのは4号墳、5号墳、6号墳、9号墳の4基のみである。その全てが地面を掘り下げて川原石を積み上げて造られた石室である。
[[発掘調査]]時には既に[[畑]]として利用されていたため、石室の石などは取り除かれていた箇所も見られた。
[[江戸時代]]後期の『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』には「古墳が[[元禄]]の頃には40～50基あったが、14、5基に減少した」とされている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 上和田村 小名 塚原}}。周辺はほとんどが未調査の区域であるため、今後の調査で古墳数はさらに増えると考えられている。しかし、この地区は現在住宅が多く建てられており、今後の調査には影響が出ると思われる。
周辺には[[稲荷塚古墳]]<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=多摩市役所教育部教育振興課文化財係|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=多摩市の文化財（稲荷塚古墳）|work=多摩市|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>、[[臼井塚古墳]]、[[庚申塚古墳]]、[[大栗川]]対岸には[[中和田横穴墓群]]や[[日野市]][[万蔵院台古墳群]]があるが、古墳以外に古墳時代の住居跡や[[土器]]なども多数検出されている。都内でも有数の古墳群の1つである。
[[多摩センター駅]]にある[[パルテノン多摩]]の歴史ミュージアムでは塚原古墳群のパネルによる紹介と、主に5号墳から出土した実物の[[鉄鏃]]、[[刀子]]、[[金環]]、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]や[[直刀|大刀]]の復元モデルなどが展示されている。
== 各古墳の発掘状況 == ===1号墳=== 直径9メートル、高さ2メートルの円墳である。10基の中で最も保存状態が良く墳丘が残っている唯一の古墳である。現在、未調査。[[羨道]]の門柱石と思われる石材が露出している。
===2号墳=== 推定直径が約19メートルの墳丘と直径約25メートルの周溝を持つ円墳。築造時期は[[6世紀]]後半。中心の墓坑（墓穴）は未調査ではあるが追葬用と思われる土坑が2基ある。周溝内から6世紀後半の[[土師器]]甕が出土した。
===3号墳=== 推定直径が約10メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。東側は二重周溝となっている。築造時期は6世紀中頃。
===4号墳=== 推定直径が約14メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。築造時期は[[7世紀]]前半。川原石を用いた半地下式の[[横穴式石室]]である（胴張り単室）。石室の両側壁は長さ30～40センチメートル、奥壁は長さ60センチメートル前後の石で築造されており高さは1メートルほどまである。[[1963年]]（昭和38年）に発掘調査が行われ、その際[[副葬品]]として大刀3本、[[鉄鏃]]数十本、刀子、金属製[[耳飾り]]、青銅製釧一対、碧玉製[[勾玉]]・[[管玉]]・[[丸玉]]、ガラス製小玉などが出土している。その副葬品は現在[[玉川大学]]に所蔵されている。
===5号墳=== 推定直径が約14メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。築造時期は6世紀第4四半期（575年-600年）。川原石を用いた[[羨道]]と[[玄室]]がある横穴式石室である（無袖式長方形単室）。[[副葬品]]として玄室内から大刀3本、[[短刀]]1本、[[刀子]]4本、[[鉄鏃]]約70本、石室周辺から[[土師器]]碗1点、[[須恵器]]坏1点・[[高坏]]5点・蓋3点・横瓶1点・短頸壺1点・甕12点などが出土している。
===6号墳=== 推定直径が約18メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。築造時期は7世紀前半。胴張複室構造をもつ横穴式石室で地下に掘り込んである。石室の床には小円礫が敷き詰められている。[[副葬品]]として鉄製[[直刀]]が1本、鉄製[[刀子]]1本、金銅製耳環2点が出土している。耳環には赤色顔料が付着していた。
===7号墳=== 石室の残骸のみが検出されており墳丘や周溝は確認されていない。
===8号墳=== 推定直径が約14メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳と思われる。築造時期は6世紀第4四半期（575年-600年）。周溝の一部が確認されている。須恵器模倣坏が出土している。
===9号墳=== 石室（片袖式単室）のみが検出されており墳丘や周溝は確認されていない。築造時期は7世紀中頃。
===10号墳=== 推定直径が約18メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳と思われる。周溝の一部（約6分の1）が確認されている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}

*{{Cite book|和書|author=多摩市遺跡調査会|title=東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳|series=多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39|publisher=[[多摩市]]|year=1996|date=1996|ncid=BA47841307|ref=harv}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=塚原古墳群 |別名= |画像={{画像募集中|cat=多摩市}} |画像キャプション= |所在地=[[東京都]][[多摩市]][[和田 (多摩市)|和田]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=[[6世紀]]中頃から[[7世紀]]中頃 |出土品=武器・武具・装身具・土師器・須恵器など |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財=未指定 |規模=[[円墳]]10基以上 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=塚原古墳群|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=都内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''塚原古墳群'''（つかっぱらこふんぐん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]][[和田 (多摩市)|和田]]にある[[古墳時代]]後期（[[6世紀]]中頃から[[7世紀]]中頃）の[[古墳群]]である。多くの[[副葬品]]が出土している。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期の古墳群で、現在10基の[[古墳]]が確認されている。現在、1号墳以外は墳丘は残っておらず、調査は[[石室]]、[[周溝]]の[[発掘調査]]となっている。埋葬者は不明である。
全てが[[横穴式石室]]による[[円墳]]と推測されるが、石室が確認されているのは4号墳、5号墳、6号墳、9号墳の4基のみである。その全てが地面を掘り下げて川原石を積み上げて造られた石室である。
[[発掘調査]]時には既に[[畑]]として利用されていたため、石室の石などは取り除かれていた箇所も見られた。
[[江戸時代]]後期の『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』には「古墳が[[元禄]]の頃には40～50基あったが、14、5基に減少した」とされている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 上和田村 小名 塚原}}。周辺はほとんどが未調査の区域であるため、今後の調査で古墳数はさらに増えると考えられている。しかし、この地区は現在住宅が多く建てられており、今後の調査には影響が出ると思われる。
周辺には[[稲荷塚古墳]]<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=多摩市役所教育部教育振興課文化財係|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=多摩市の文化財（稲荷塚古墳）|work=多摩市|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>、[[臼井塚古墳]]、[[庚申塚古墳]]、[[大栗川]]対岸には[[中和田横穴墓群]]や[[日野市]][[万蔵院台古墳群]]があるが、古墳以外に古墳時代の住居跡や[[土器]]なども多数検出されている。都内でも有数の古墳群の1つである。
[[多摩センター駅]]にある[[パルテノン多摩]]の歴史ミュージアムでは塚原古墳群のパネルによる紹介と、主に5号墳から出土した実物の[[鉄鏃]]、[[刀子]]、[[金環]]、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]や[[直刀|大刀]]の復元モデルなどが展示されている。
== 各古墳の発掘状況 == ===1号墳=== 直径9メートル、高さ2メートルの円墳である。10基の中で最も保存状態が良く墳丘が残っている唯一の古墳である。現在、未調査。[[羨道]]の門柱石と思われる石材が露出している。
===2号墳=== 推定直径が約19メートルの墳丘と直径約25メートルの周溝を持つ円墳。築造時期は[[6世紀]]後半。中心の墓坑（墓穴）は未調査ではあるが追葬用と思われる土坑が2基ある。周溝内から6世紀後半の[[土師器]]甕が出土した。
===3号墳=== 推定直径が約10メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。東側は二重周溝となっている。築造時期は6世紀中頃。
===4号墳=== 推定直径が約14メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。築造時期は[[7世紀]]前半。川原石を用いた半地下式の[[横穴式石室]]である（胴張り単室）。石室の両側壁は長さ30～40センチメートル、奥壁は長さ60センチメートル前後の石で築造されており高さは1メートルほどまである。[[1963年]]（昭和38年）に発掘調査が行われ、その際[[副葬品]]として大刀3本、[[鉄鏃]]数十本、刀子、金属製[[耳飾り]]、青銅製釧一対、碧玉製[[勾玉]]・[[管玉]]・[[丸玉]]、ガラス製小玉などが出土している。その副葬品は現在[[玉川大学]]に所蔵されている。
===5号墳=== 推定直径が約14メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。築造時期は6世紀第4四半期（575年-600年）。川原石を用いた[[羨道]]と[[玄室]]がある横穴式石室である（無袖式長方形単室）。[[副葬品]]として玄室内から大刀3本、[[短刀]]1本、[[刀子]]4本、[[鉄鏃]]約70本、石室周辺から[[土師器]]碗1点、[[須恵器]]坏1点・[[高坏]]5点・蓋3点・横瓶1点・短頸壺1点・甕12点などが出土している。
===6号墳=== 推定直径が約18メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳。築造時期は7世紀前半。胴張複室構造をもつ横穴式石室で地下に掘り込んである。石室の床には小円礫が敷き詰められている。[[副葬品]]として鉄製[[直刀]]が1本、鉄製[[刀子]]1本、金銅製耳環2点が出土している。耳環には赤色顔料が付着していた。
===7号墳=== 石室の残骸のみが検出されており墳丘や周溝は確認されていない。
===8号墳=== 推定直径が約14メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳と思われる。築造時期は6世紀第4四半期（575年-600年）。周溝の一部が確認されている。須恵器模倣坏が出土している。
===9号墳=== 石室（片袖式単室）のみが検出されており墳丘や周溝は確認されていない。築造時期は7世紀中頃。
===10号墳=== 推定直径が約18メートルの墳丘を持つ円墳と思われる。周溝の一部（約6分の1）が確認されている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ98多磨郡ノ10|chapter=上和田村|id={{NDLJP|763990/13}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上和田村 小名 塚原}}}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=多摩市史編集委員会|title=多摩市史 資料編1（考古・古代・中世）|publisher=[[多摩市]]|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=多摩市遺跡調査会|title=東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳|series=多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39|publisher=[[多摩市]]|year=1996|date=1996|ncid=BA47841307|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|author=多摩市遺跡調査会|title=東京多摩市 和田・百草遺跡群、塚原古墳群|series=多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告43|publisher=[[多摩市]]|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}

|map={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}}

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|qq=|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|qq=|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|qq=|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. Numerous [[:en:Grave goods|grave goods]] have been unearthed here.

== Overview ==
The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.

All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.

Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.

According to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.

Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Hino, Tokyo|Hino City]]. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near [[:en:Tama-Center Station|Tama-Center Station]], displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed [[:en:大刀|Tachi sword]] model from Mound 5.

== Excavation Details for Each Mound ==
=== Mound No. 1 ===
A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.

=== Mound No. 2 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.

=== Mound No. 3 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.

=== Mound No. 4 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]].

=== Mound No. 5 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.

=== Mound No. 6 ===
A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.

=== Mound No. 7 ===
Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.

=== Mound No. 8 ===
A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.

=== Mound No. 9 ===
A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.

=== Mound No. 10 ===
A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
