<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image requested|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}} |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|35.64355555555556|N|139.43294444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |excavations=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji pottery, Sue pottery, etc. |Burial Facility={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Historic Site=None |Historic Site=None |area=Over 10 {{ill|Circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}. Numerous {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=grave goods}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Hino, Tokyo|en|Hino, Tokyo|WD=Q236197|lt=Hino City}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|en|Tama-Center Station|WD=Q988468|lt=Tama-Center Station}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi sword}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|en|Tamagawa University|WD=Q7680543|lt=Tamagawa University}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City Archaeological Survey Team|title=Tokyo Tama City Archaeological Sites: Wada-Mogusa Sites and Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Tama City|year=1998|date=1998|ncid=BA47842740|ref=harv}}

However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Hino, Tokyo|en|Hino, Tokyo|WD=Q236197|lt=Hino City}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image requested|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}} |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|35.64355555555556|N|139.43294444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |excavations=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji pottery, Sue pottery, etc. |Burial Facility={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Historic Site=None |Historic Site=None |area=Over 10 {{ill|Circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}. Numerous {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=grave goods}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Hino, Tokyo|en|Hino, Tokyo|WD=Q236197|lt=Hino City}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|en|Tama-Center Station|WD=Q988468|lt=Tama-Center Station}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi sword}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|en|Tamagawa University|WD=Q7680543|lt=Tamagawa University}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
== References == {{Reflist}}

*{{Cite book|和書|author=Tama City History Editorial Committee|title=Tama City History: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval|publisher=Tama City|year=1995|date=1995|ncid=BN1438313X|ref=harv}}

Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|en|Tamagawa University|WD=Q7680543|lt=Tamagawa University|1=Tamagawa University}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster |Other names= |image={{image requested|cat=Tama City}} |image caption= |location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}} |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|35.64355555555556|N|139.43294444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=Mid-6th to mid-7th century |excavations=Weapons, armor, jewelry, Haji pottery, Sue pottery, etc. |Burial Facility={{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Historic Site=None |Historic Site=None |area=Over 10 {{ill|Circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map={{location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=36.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=58.6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.8|N|139|25|58.6|E}}}} }}
'''Tsukahara Kofun Cluster''' (塚原古墳群) is a group of late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Wada (Tama City)|ja|和田 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada (Tama City)|1=Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869635}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}. Numerous {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been unearthed here.
== Overview == The Tsukahara Kofun Cluster is a group of Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, with 10 confirmed mounds. Except for Mound No. 1, the Kofuns have no remaining mounds, and investigations are limited to excavations of the {{ill|stone chambers|lt=stone chambers|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} and surrounding ditches. The identities of the interred remain unknown.
All mounds are presumed to be {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though stone chambers have only been confirmed for Mounds 4, 5, 6, and 9. The chambers were constructed by digging into the ground and piling river stones.
Excavations revealed that the area had been used for farming, resulting in the removal of some chamber stones.
According to the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', “40–50 Kofuns existed during the Genroku era but had decreased to 14–15 mounds by the late Edo period.”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Ko}} The area is largely uninvestigated, and further surveys could reveal more Kofuns. However, the construction of residential buildings in the area is expected to affect future investigations.
Nearby sites include {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name=Tama>{{Cite web|和書|author=Tama City Board of Education|url=http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html|title=Tama City's Cultural Properties (Inarizuka Kofun)|work=Tama City|accessdate=2020-09-22}}</ref>, {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshinzuka Kofun|lt=Kōshintzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshintzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and across the {{ill|Ōkurigawa River|ja|大栗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurigawa River|1=Ōkuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436256}}, the {{ill|Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} and the {{ill|Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja|万蔵院台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzoin-dai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Hino, Tokyo|en|Hino, Tokyo|WD=Q236197|lt=Hino City|1=Hino, Tokyo}}. Numerous artifacts from the Kofun period, including residential site remains and pottery, have also been found in the area. It is considered one of the most prominent kofun clusters in Tokyo.
The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|en|Tama-Center Station|WD=Q988468|lt=Tama-Center Station|1=Tama-Center Station}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi sword|1=大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|en|Tamagawa University|WD=Q7680543|lt=Tamagawa University|1=Tamagawa University}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.

The historical museum at {{ill|Parthenon Tama|ja|パルテノン多摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Parthenon Tama|1=Parthenon Tama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329444}}, near {{ill|Tama-Center Station|en|Tama-Center Station|WD=Q988468|lt=Tama-Center Station|1=Tama-Center Station}}, displays panels on the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster and exhibits artifacts, including iron arrowheads, small swords, metal rings, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, and a reconstructed {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi sword|1=大刀}} model from Mound 5.
== Excavation Details for Each Mound == === Mound No. 1 === A circular Kofun with a diameter of 9 meters and a height of 2 meters. It is the only mound in the cluster with a remaining mound. Uninvestigated to date, stones thought to be pillar stones for the {{ill|burial chamber|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} entrance are visible.
=== Mound No. 2 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 19 meters and a surrounding ditch of 25 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century. Two additional burial pits, likely for secondary burials, were found. A late 6th-century Haji pottery jar was unearthed from the ditch.
=== Mound No. 3 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 10 meters, featuring double surrounding ditches on the eastern side. Dated to the mid-6th century.
=== Mound No. 4 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the early 7th century, it has a semi-underground horizontal stone chamber with a single, slightly curved structure. Artifacts include three swords, numerous iron arrowheads, metal earrings, a pair of bronze armlets, and various beads. Artifacts are stored at {{ill|Tamagawa University|en|Tamagawa University|WD=Q7680543|lt=Tamagawa University|1=Tamagawa University}}.
=== Mound No. 5 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 14 meters. Constructed in the late 6th century (575–600). It features a stone chamber with a passage and a rectangular chamber. excavations include three long swords, one short sword, four knives, approximately 70 arrowheads, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery.
=== Mound No. 6 === A circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. Built in the early 7th century, it features a stone chamber with a double-curved structure. Artifacts include an iron sword, a knife, and two gold-plated copper earrings.
=== Mound No. 7 === Only stone chamber remnants were found. The Kofun and ditch were not confirmed.
=== Mound No. 8 === A probable circular Kofun with a diameter of 14 meters, built in the late 6th century (575–600). Partial ditches and Sue pottery imitations were found.
=== Mound No. 9 === A burial chamber with a single-sided structure was found. Neither the Kofun nor the ditch was confirmed. Dated to the mid-7th century.
=== Mound No. 10 === A probable circular Kofun with an estimated diameter of 18 meters. A partially confirmed ditch surrounds it.
